title: Piaropus crassipes
taxonomy: Piaropus crassipes is a species of plant in the family Euphorbiaceae, commonly known as the water hyacinth. It belongs to the genus Piaropus, which is sometimes classified under the genus Eichhornia. The water hyacinth is native to South America, but it has been widely distributed and naturalized in other parts of the world. It is considered an invasive species in many regions due to its ability to outcompete native vegetation and disrupt ecosystems. The plant's classification can vary depending on the source and taxonomic framework used.
